items._note: EAST landing wall NORTH segment (z 6..16.0833): separates the family room (west) from the walk-in closet (east). Solid. Mirrors the west landing wall's north segment.

EAST landing wall SOUTH segment (z -11.9167..-1): separates the landing/hall from the primary bedroom. Solid. The z -1..6 gap between the segments is the OPEN MOUTH of the east central hall - mirror of the west alcove mouth (the old single primary-entry door is replaced by this open mouth).

Walk-in divider along z=6 (x -12..3.9167). Its WEST 5 ft (x -1.0833..3.9167) is the NORTH wall of the east central hall; the door here is the hall->walk-in entry (mirrors the west alcove's NW-bedroom door: 36" leaf, hinge W, opens N). West of x -1.0833 the divider is solid, so the walk-in is reached ONLY from the hall, not through the bedroom.
EAST CENTRAL HALL south wall (z=-1, x -1.0833..3.9167): 5 ft wall with a 36" door into the PRIMARY BEDROOM (mirrors the west alcove's SW-bedroom door). Hinge W, opens S into the bedroom.
EAST CENTRAL HALL back (east) wall (x=-1.0833, z -1..6): solid. Mirrors the J&J bath east wall that closes the west alcove. The primary bedroom wraps east and south of the hall.
Primary <-> en-suite (extension) separator at x=-12; door from the primary bedroom into the en-suite.
West landing wall NORTH segment (z 6..16.0833): family-room + NW-bedroom east wall. Solid.

West landing wall SOUTH segment (z -11.9167..-1): SW-bedroom east wall (backs the stair). Solid. The z -1..5 gap between the segments is OPEN — the old central hall is now merged into the E-W hall/landing.
NW bedroom / alcove divider (z=6). The 5 ft alcove wall (x 15.0833..20.0833): a 36" door centered with 12" of wall each side. Opens N into the NW bedroom.
SW bedroom / alcove divider (z=-1). The 5 ft alcove wall: a 36" door centered with 12" of wall each side. Opens S into the SW bedroom.

J&J BATH east wall (x=20.0833, z -1..6): solid. The bath's N-S span now matches the alcove's (z -1..6), so it sits directly beside the alcove and the bedrooms are clean rectangles. Bath reached ONLY through the bedrooms.
J&J BATH north wall (z=6, x 20.0833..31): door into the NW bedroom (opens N into the bedroom).
J&J BATH south wall (z=-1, x 20.0833..31): door into the SW bedroom (opens S into the bedroom).

FAMILY ROOM south wall = north wall of the E-W hall (z=6, aligned with the NW-bedroom south wall to make one continuous line across the floor). Door opens NORTH into the family room; the hall is south of it.
PRIMARY BEDROOM king (SOUTH room): headboard on the NORTH wall (walk-in divider, no window); foot points SOUTH to the window wall (no door). Clears the entry, en-suite, and walk-in doors.
items.head: N

items._note: SW BEDROOM queen (room x 15.0833..31, z -11.9167..-1, a clean rectangle): headboard on the EAST (landing) wall - interior, no window; foot points WEST toward the window wall (no door). Centered z ~ -6.5. Clears the bath's south door and the alcove door.
items.head: E

items._note: NW BEDROOM queen (room x 15.0833..31, z 6..16.0833, a clean rectangle): headboard on the EAST (landing) wall - interior, no window; foot points WEST toward the window wall (no door). Centered z ~ 11.0. Clears the bath's north door.
items.head: E

items._note: J&J BATH (x 20.0833..31, z -1..6 = 10.9 x 7 ft). The west wet wall is only 7 ft N-S - too short for all three fixtures stacked - so shower (N) + single vanity (S, under the west window ~z2.08) sit on the wet wall (x=31), and the toilet goes on the interior EAST wall (x=20.0833), clear of the two bedroom doors on the N/S walls (both near x24).
PRIMARY EN-SUITE (extension x -22.92..-12, z -11.92..4): vanity on the north wall, shower SW, toilet on the separator wall.
FAMILY ROOM (landing north bay, x 3.9167..15.0833, z 5..16.0833): sofa against the west wall facing the TV on the east wall, with a rug between.
